title: Living in Boulder vlog 5 - 11 - 2017: Collecting Ancient Classics for my website

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a brief vlog update about staying up until 5am collecting and organizing ancient classic books (Aristotle, Roman/Byzantine history) for his website. He describes his goal of aggregating as many old books as possible onto one page and plans to continue through the following Sunday.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: no single site has all the old books and none of them look good
excited and motivated, working extremely hard to compile as many historical texts as possible

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got up late today because I was working until around 5 in the morning.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I woke up at around 11 or 12.

claims.subject: Aristotle
claims.claim: I was studying Aristotle last night.

claims.subject: books
claims.claim: Around 400 AD, there are a lot of books from that time.

claims.subject: Roman Empire
claims.claim: I was researching the Roman Empire and Byzantine Empire today.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I set up a list of about 30 books in my bookmarks.

claims.claim: There's no single site that has every old book; they're scattered across multiple sites.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm working hard despite being high.

claims.subject: computer
claims.claim: I wish I had a faster computer for research purposes.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I lost some opportunities due to my current computer.

claims.claim: It's amazing to compile every book in history together.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to work on this project until next Sunday.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I might go into a 'crazy mode' and switch to something else.

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder V-Log for May 11th, 2017.
So today, I got up kind of late because I was working until like 4, like 5 in the morning or something crazy like that.
So yeah, I got up at like 11, I mean 12, something like that.
And so this, yesterday I was doing, I guess, Aristotle.
Last night, it's hard to remember because I'm doing random stuff, church stuff, right around the 400s.
There's a lot of books from that time, like at the end of the Roman Empire.
I'm doing Aristotle right now though.
And then I'm going to, I set up a whole list on my bookmarks of like 30 or something books.
So I'm just going to blitzkrieg this week.
But I think I can get most of them.
For some reason, there's no site that has every old book on it.
There's just a bunch of ones that have a few and none of them are really organized well.
So it would be cool.
I can't wait to find out what it's going to look like, all of them together on one page.
So what am I listening to?
I was doing actually Wikipedia stuff today.
Having to do with the Roman, Roman political stuff and the Byzantine Empire.
But it's kind of hard to remember the details because I'm so high.
But I'm working like a motherfucker.
Man, I wish I had a faster computer before.
I'm going to do research and find out like how long these fast computers are around.
Like how much this computer, a computer this good was like 10 years ago.
Just out of curiosity.
Let's see if I lost any opportunities or whatever.
But it's amazing to feel it just come together.
You can put like every book in history together in just a couple weeks.
I could get, and that would be until the end of the Roman.
I mean, maybe later I'll do from then all the way up until the 1800s or something.
But, yeah, that's really all.
There's nothing new.
I know I'm spending all my time on that.
But next Sunday, my idea was to work until next Sunday and then tie them together.
Put them on my website.
Just see how much I could collect until then.
Unless I'm like in some crazy mode where I'm like, ooh, let's go for something else.
Or if I go one more week, I can finish.
So we'll see.
So anyways, that's enough talking for today and my exciting life.
Ooh, three minutes.
